visa_policy.notes: Zimbabwe uses a three-category visa regime as published on the official evisa.gov.zw portal and confirmed on zimimmigration.gov.zw. Category A nationals do not require a visa ('Entry without Visa'). Category B nationals can obtain a visa on arrival or apply in advance via the e-visa portal at evisa.gov.zw. Category C nationals must obtain a visa before travelling - available via the online e-visa portal. The e-visa portal offers: Single Entry, Double Entry, Multiple Entry, and KAZA UNIVISA. As of June 2026, evisa.gov.zw/faq.html no longer publicly lists visa fees; the official fee schedule is published at zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958. Maximum stay durations for Category A (visa-free) are not explicitly stated on any official source page. Note: nationality-specific fees apply - UK/British nationals pay USD 55 (single) / USD 70 (double) online; Chinese nationals pay USD 60 (single) online; standard single entry is USD 30.

visa_types.name: Single Entry Visa (Holiday)
visa_types.purpose: For visitors travelling to Zimbabwe for leisure, tourism, or holiday purposes. Valid for a single entry.
visa_types.notes: Available as e-visa (Category B and C countries) or visa on arrival (Category B countries only). Standard fee USD 30 (ordinary). Nationality-specific fees apply: UK/British nationals pay USD 55 online; Chinese nationals pay USD 60 online. Category A nationals do not require a visa. Fee schedule confirmed at zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958; evisa.gov.zw/faq.html no longer publicly lists fees as of June 2026.

visa_types.name: Double Entry Visa (Holiday)
visa_types.purpose: For visitors wishing to make two entries into Zimbabwe within the validity period, e.g. for a trip that crosses into a neighbouring country and returns.
visa_types.notes: Two entries permitted within 3-month validity. Available as e-visa online or visa on arrival for Category B countries. Standard fee USD 45. Nationality-specific fee: UK/British nationals pay USD 70 online. Fee schedule confirmed at zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958; evisa.gov.zw/faq.html no longer publicly lists fees as of June 2026.

visa_types.name: Multiple Entry Visa (Holiday)
visa_types.purpose: For visitors requiring multiple entries into Zimbabwe over the validity period.
visa_types.notes: Fee updated to USD 55 per zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958 (re-verified June 2026). Previous record showed USD 160 - that figure was sourced from zimembassydc.org and evisa.gov.zw FAQ (when it still listed fees). evisa.gov.zw FAQ no longer publicly lists fees. validity_days set to null pending official re-confirmation; previous entries showed 6 months (evisa.gov.zw) or 12 months (zimembassydc.org); zimimmigration fees page does not state validity period.

visa_types.name: KAZA UNIVISA
visa_types.purpose: A joint visa permitting entry into both Zimbabwe and Zambia (and day visits to Botswana via the Kazungula border post) within the Kavango-Zambezi Transfrontier Conservation Area.
visa_types.notes: KAZA UNIVISA is jointly issued by Zimbabwe and Zambia. It allows multiple entries across the KAZA conservation area. Fee is USD 50 (not listed on the zimimmigration fees page; fee retained from previous evisa.gov.zw source). Available as e-visa via evisa.gov.zw or on arrival at designated border posts. Only available to eligible nationalities (not all nationalities qualify for KAZA).

visa_types.name: Emergency Visa (Online)
visa_types.purpose: For urgent travel to Zimbabwe where normal processing timelines cannot be met.
visa_types.notes: Emergency online visa at USD 100, confirmed on zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958 fee schedule (re-verified June 2026). Eligibility criteria, max stay duration, and processing time not stated on the official fee page. Available only online via evisa.gov.zw.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals travelling to Zimbabwe for business-related activities such as meetings, negotiations, and seminars, without entering the local workforce.
visa_types.notes: Business visa does not permit the holder to take up employment in Zimbabwe. Category B nationals may obtain on arrival; Category C nationals must apply via evisa.gov.zw in advance. Same fee structure as the holiday visa applies (USD 30 single, USD 45 double, USD 160 multiple entry).

visa_types.name: Conferencing Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals attending conferences, seminars, workshops, or professional events in Zimbabwe.
visa_types.notes: Conferencing visa is specifically for attendance at official events. Holder may not take up employment. Available via e-visa portal or on arrival depending on nationality category.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals passing through Zimbabwe to a third destination, without intending to remain in the country.
visa_types.notes: Transit visa allows passage through Zimbabwe only; the holder may not stay. Specific fee and maximum transit duration were not stated on the official zimimmigration.gov.zw page at time of research. Available via e-visa portal or on arrival depending on nationality.

visa_types.name: Temporary Employment Permit
visa_types.purpose: Allows skilled foreign nationals to work in Zimbabwe for a specific employer where no suitable local candidate is available.
visa_types.notes: Non-refundable application fee of USD 500; USD 300 per dependent. Requires 15 documents including IF5 form, employment contract, notarised academic and professional certificates, police clearance (max 6 months old), medical radiological certificate, company incorporation documents, and proof that the role was advertised locally. Renewals require a letter to the Chief Director of Immigration. Applied for via the e-permits portal at epermits.zimimmigration.gov.zw.

visa_types.name: Student Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled at an accredited educational institution in Zimbabwe for formal study.
visa_types.notes: Student permit is listed on the official Zimbabwe Department of Immigration website. Specific fees and duration are not published on the accessible official pages at time of research. Applications are made via epermits.zimimmigration.gov.zw.

visa_types.name: Scholars Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals attending school (primary or secondary level) at an institution in Zimbabwe.
visa_types.notes: Scholars permit is listed separately from the student permit on the official Zimbabwe Department of Immigration website, suggesting it covers pre-tertiary education. Fees and duration not published on accessible official pages. Applications via epermits.zimimmigration.gov.zw.

visa_types.name: Spouse Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who are spouses of Zimbabwean citizens or permanent residents, allowing lawful residence in Zimbabwe.
visa_types.notes: Spouse Residence Permit is listed on the official Zimbabwe Department of Immigration website. Specific fees and duration are not published on accessible official pages at time of research.

visa_types.name: Investor Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who invest in Zimbabwe's economy, granting lawful residence tied to their ongoing investment.
visa_types.notes: Issued for 12 months, renewable annually. Non-refundable application fee of USD 500; USD 300 per dependent. Required documents include IF5 form, project proposal, police clearance (max 6 months old), medical radiological certificate, banking history, and proof of funds. The minimum investment threshold is not stated on the official page at time of research - previously cited figures (USD 100,000 or USD 1,000,000) could not be confirmed from the official source. Applications via epermits.zimimmigration.gov.zw.

visa_types.name: Permanent Residence Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals who have satisfied the residency requirements and wish to permanently reside in Zimbabwe.
visa_types.notes: Permanent Residence Permit is listed on the official Zimbabwe Department of Immigration website. Eligibility criteria, minimum residency period, and fees are not published on the accessible official pages at time of research. Applications via epermits.zimimmigration.gov.zw.

cbi.notes: No official citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program found on any official Zimbabwe government source. In November 2022 the Zimbabwean government announced plans to create a CBI pathway (per Xinhua/official statements), but no program has been legislated or officially launched as of research date. No CBI program is evidenced on evisa.gov.zw, zimimmigration.gov.zw, or zimfa.gov.zw.

programs.name: Investor Residence Permit
programs.notes: The Investor Residence Permit is issued for 12 months (renewable annually). Application fee: USD 500 (non-refundable); USD 300 per dependent. Required documents include a completed I.F.5 form, project proposal, police clearance (not older than six months), medical radiological certificate, banking history and proof of funds. The minimum investment threshold (previously cited as USD 100,000 or USD 1,000,000 depending on track) could NOT be confirmed from the official zimimmigration.gov.zw page, which was directly accessed in this validation. Those thresholds have been removed from structured fields pending official confirmation. Path to permanent residence and citizenship years are not stated on the official page.

programs.name: Temporary Employment Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals offered employment by a Zimbabwean employer who cannot find a suitable local candidate for the role
programs.notes: The Temporary Employment Permit allows skilled foreign nationals to work in Zimbabwe. Application fee: USD 500 (non-refundable); USD 300 per dependent. Requires 15 documents including a completed I.F.5 form, contract of employment, notarized academic and professional certificates, police clearance, medical certificate, company incorporation documents, and proof of local advertisement for the role. Processing time not specified on the official page. Renewals require an application letter to the Chief Director of Immigration.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of Indian diplomatic passports may enter Zimbabwe visa-free for up to 90 days under the bilateral Agreement on Exemption from Visa Requirements for Holders of Diplomatic Passports between the Republic of India and the Republic of Zimbabwe, signed 03 November 2018, entered into force 30 October 2020. Service and official passports are NOT covered - only diplomatic passports.

Holders of Chinese diplomatic or service passports may enter Zimbabwe visa-free for up to 60 days under the Mutual Visa Exemption MOU between the Government of the People's Republic of China and the Government of the Republic of Zimbabwe for holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports, effective 12 November 2014. Stays beyond 60 days require registration with competent authorities.

agreements: SADC (Southern African Development Community) - member state; SADC Protocol on Facilitation of Movement of Persons signed but not fully ratified across all member states

validation_notes: ADDED: Zambia (ZMB) to visa_free (Category A) - confirmed on zimimmigration.gov.zw (?p=7176); Zambia does NOT appear on evisa.gov.zw/faq.html (discrepancy between the two official sources noted).

CORRECTED: max_stay_days for all visa_on_arrival (Category B) entries updated from null to 30 - US State Dept travel page for Zimbabwe confirms single-entry VoA is 30 days for USD 30; zimembassydc.org confirms single-entry USD 30, double-entry USD 45, multiple-entry USD 160 (12 months).

CORRECTED: rbi.programs[0].min_amount and rbi.programs[0].currency changed from 100000/USD to null/null - the official zimimmigration.gov.zw investor permit page (?p=7282), now directly accessible, does not state a minimum investment amount. The previously cited thresholds (USD 100,000 / USD 1,000,000) were unconfirmed; removed from structured fields.

CORRECTED: fast_track.programs[0].notes updated to include confirmed fee information (USD 500 application, USD 300 per dependent) and document list per the now-accessible zimimmigration.gov.zw (?p=7338).
CORRECTED: cbi.notes updated - removed reference to zimimmigration.gov.zw domain being compromised (site is now restored and accessible).
CORRECTED: visa_policy.notes updated - zimimmigration.gov.zw is now restored and accessible; updated notes to reflect this change from previous research session.
ADDED: zimimmigration.gov.zw to official_domains (was omitted in prior record despite being a primary official source).

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: All Category A (visa_free) entries except Zambia remain consistent between evisa.gov.zw/faq.html and zimimmigration.gov.zw. All Category B (visa_on_arrival) and Category C (e_visa) country lists are unchanged from prior record - confirmed against live evisa.gov.zw/faq.html.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: conditional_access entries for India (diplomatic passport, 90 days) and China (diplomatic+service passport, 60 days) verified against source URLs; no change required.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: evisa.gov.zw/faq.html re-fetched - Category A list confirmed matches current visa_free entries (same 45 entries minus Zambia which is on zimimmigration only). Category B list confirmed matches current visa_on_arrival entries. Category C list confirmed matches current e_visa entries. No additions or removals required from any access tier.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7176 (Category A) re-fetched - 45 entries confirmed; Zambia present; Aruba NOT on this list (Aruba is on evisa.gov.zw Category A only). Aruba retained in visa_free with evisa.gov.zw as source; discrepancy logged in gaps.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7186 (Category B) re-fetched - list broadly consistent with current visa_on_arrival entries. Two discrepancies noted: Bolivia and South Sudan appear in zimimmigration Category B but are classified Category C (e_visa) per evisa.gov.zw/faq.html. Conservative safety rule applied - kept as e_visa (stricter classification); discrepancy logged in gaps. Australia and Liechtenstein appear in evisa.gov.zw Category B but NOT in zimimmigration Category B; kept in visa_on_arrival (confirmed by evisa.gov.zw); discrepancy logged in gaps.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7196 (Category C) re-fetched for first time - list largely consistent with current e_visa entries. Anguilla and East Timor (Timor-Leste) are NOT on zimimmigration Category C but ARE on evisa.gov.zw Category C; retained in e_visa with evisa.gov.zw as source; discrepancy logged in gaps.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: evisa.gov.zw/faq.html re-fetched - Category A, B, C lists confirmed unchanged from previous validation. All 45 visa_free entries (incl. Aruba from evisa.gov.zw; excl. Zambia which is zimimmigration.gov.zw only), all visa_on_arrival entries, and all e_visa entries match exactly. No changes to nationality access tiers required.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7176 (Category A), ?p=7186 (Category B), ?p=7196 (Category C) re-fetched - lists consistent with previous validation. No new additions or removals detected.

CORRECTED 2026-06-29: visa_types fee structure updated per zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958. Key changes: (1) Single Entry Visa now notes nationality-specific fees - UK/British USD 55, Chinese USD 60, ordinary USD 30. (2) Double Entry Visa notes UK/British fee of USD 70. (3) Multiple Entry Visa fee changed from USD 160 to USD 55 per current official fee schedule; validity_days set to null as zimimmigration.gov.zw does not state validity on the fees page. (4) Emergency Visa (Online) USD 100 added as a new visa_type - confirmed on zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958.

NOTED 2026-06-29: evisa.gov.zw/faq.html no longer publicly lists visa fees; the page now states 'The actual cost of your visa will be stated in your approval response.' The current official fee schedule is at zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED 2026-06-29: conditional_access entries (India diplomatic 90 days; China diplomatic+service 60 days) not re-fetched this run; no new conflicting information found. Retained as-is per previous validation.
gaps: Maximum stay days for Category A (visa-free) are not explicitly stated on any official Zimbabwe government source (evisa.gov.zw or zimimmigration.gov.zw).
Maximum stay days for Category C (e-visa, pre-travel) entries are not confirmed from the official evisa.gov.zw FAQ page.

Investor Residence Permit minimum investment thresholds: the official zimimmigration.gov.zw page (?p=7282) does not state a minimum amount; previously cited USD 100,000 / USD 1,000,000 thresholds could not be re-confirmed and have been removed from structured fields.
No official CBI program exists or was found; only an announcement of intent was made in 2022 - no official program details are available.
Path to permanent residence and citizenship years for the Investor Residence Permit could not be confirmed from official sources.

Zambia discrepancy: Zambia appears on zimimmigration.gov.zw Category A page but NOT on evisa.gov.zw/faq.html. Both are official Zimbabwe government sources. Added to visa_free with the zimimmigration.gov.zw source; discrepancy noted.

Aruba discrepancy (noted 2026-06-27): Aruba appears on evisa.gov.zw/faq.html Category A but NOT on zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7176 Category A. Retained in visa_free with evisa.gov.zw as source; cannot confirm or disprove from zimimmigration alone.

Bolivia discrepancy (noted 2026-06-27): Bolivia appears in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7186 Category B (visa on arrival) but evisa.gov.zw/faq.html lists Bolivia under Category C (must obtain visa before travel). Current record keeps Bolivia in e_visa following the evisa.gov.zw classification (stricter/safer). Bolivia also does NOT appear in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7196 Category C, deepening the discrepancy. Cannot resolve without further official clarification.

South Sudan discrepancy (noted 2026-06-27): South Sudan appears in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7186 Category B (visa on arrival) but evisa.gov.zw/faq.html lists South Sudan under Category C (must obtain visa before travel). Current record keeps South Sudan in e_visa following the evisa.gov.zw classification (stricter/safer). South Sudan also does NOT appear in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7196 Category C, deepening the discrepancy.

Australia discrepancy (noted 2026-06-27): Australia appears in evisa.gov.zw/faq.html Category B (visa on arrival) but is NOT listed in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7186 Category B. Retained in visa_on_arrival (confirmed by evisa.gov.zw).

Liechtenstein discrepancy (noted 2026-06-27): Liechtenstein appears in evisa.gov.zw/faq.html Category B (visa on arrival) but is NOT listed in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7186 Category B. Retained in visa_on_arrival (confirmed by evisa.gov.zw).

Anguilla discrepancy (noted 2026-06-27): Anguilla appears in evisa.gov.zw/faq.html Category C (e_visa, pre-travel) but is NOT listed in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7196 Category C. Retained in e_visa (confirmed by evisa.gov.zw).

East Timor / Timor-Leste discrepancy (noted 2026-06-27): East Timor appears in evisa.gov.zw/faq.html Category C (e_visa, pre-travel) but is NOT listed in zimimmigration.gov.zw/?p=7196 Category C. Retained in e_visa (confirmed by evisa.gov.zw).
Digital-nomad or startup visa schemes were not found on any accessible official Zimbabwe government source.

SADC Protocol on Facilitation of Movement of Persons: Zimbabwe has signed but the protocol is not fully operationalized. Specific ratification status of Zimbabwe not confirmed from the official SADC or Zimbabwe government page directly.

Conditional access - credential-based: No official Zimbabwe government source was found confirming visa-free or VoA entry for holders of US, UK, Schengen, or other third-country visas or residence permits.
Conditional access - special status: No official source found for Zimbabwe recognising India OCI cards or any diaspora/heritage status as a basis for conditional entry.

Conditional access - diplomatic passport waivers: Additional bilateral agreements beyond India and China may exist (e.g. Russia, various SADC states) but could not be confirmed from official sources accessible at time of research.

The China NIA source (en.nia.gov.cn) lists Zimbabwe's mutual visa exemption as covering diplomatic and service passports for 60 days effective 2014-11-12; the separate Chinese MFA page (cs.mfa.gov.cn) lists 30 days. The more specific NIA source's 60-day figure is used; discrepancy noted.

Multiple Entry Visa validity discrepancy (noted 2026-06-29): zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958 shows fee of USD 55 but does not state validity period. Previous sources showed 6 months (evisa.gov.zw) or 12 months (zimembassydc.org). validity_days set to null; fee updated to USD 55 per official fee page.
Emergency Visa (Online) details incomplete (noted 2026-06-29): zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958 shows fee of USD 100 but does not state max stay duration, validity period, or eligibility criteria.

UK/British and Chinese nationality-specific fee structure (noted 2026-06-29): zimimmigration.gov.zw/?page_id=6958 shows nationality-specific fees (UK: USD 55 single / USD 70 double; Chinese: USD 60 single) confirmed. Whether these nationality-specific fees also apply to visa-on-arrival at port of entry (vs online only) is not confirmed from the official source.

outbound_official.note: no official Zimbabwe government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data
